opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I caught a black mole on my back with the towel getting out of the shower and now it's bleeding everywhere. It's raised and dark and I think it's got bigger over the last while — I'm terrified it's cancer.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It happened just now while I was drying off, there's blood on the towel and I panicked. I need someone to tell me whether it's serious or get it sorted tonight.

symptom_narrative.onset: this evening, when I snagged the mole with a towel after a shower
symptom_narrative.duration: bleeding for the last little while since I caught it
symptom_narrative.character: a raised, dark, almost black mole on my upper back; the top got partly torn off and it's oozing blood; I feel like it's grown over the past few months
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: it feels like an emergency to me, I'm really frightened
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: on my back near the right shoulder blade, where I can't see it well
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: started bleeding the moment I snagged it and has kept spotting since
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: pressing a towel against it slows the bleeding
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: touching it or rubbing it with clothing
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: my heart is racing and I feel keyed up and panicky
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: it has never bled before but I've noticed it for a while and thought it looked different lately

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: bad blistering sunburns as a teenager

family_history_as_reported.narrative: no family history of melanoma or skin cancer that I know of

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: spouse is present and very supportive; patient is extremely anxious about skin cancer tonight

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants immediate certainty that it is not cancer, or immediate removal tonight

objective_data_offered.value: a raised dark, almost black mole about a centimetre across on the right upper back, with the top partly torn off and oozing blood; blood spots on a towel held against it
spouse says he is pacing, very distressed, refusing to put a shirt on because of the bleeding, and holding a blood-spotted towel to his back
he says his heart feels like it's racing and he feels shaky and panicky
